channel.title: Frontaliere Ticino — English
channel.description: News and guides for cross-border workers in Ticino

item.title: Switzerland hosts the \
item.description: Canton Ticino becomes the \

item.title: Switzerland: Slower Growth and Mild Inflation Increase
item.description: Switzerland is expected to see economic growth of 1% in 2026, with inflation slightly rising. What does this mean for Italian border workers?

item.title: Roadworks: Night Closures at Agrate, Cavenago, and Monza
item.description: Night closures will be implemented to allow for inspection and maintenance work on the highway network. Here are the schedules and reasons.

item.title: Car-Free Sundays in Switzerland: Is Ticino Moving Towards New Mobility?
item.description: Martine Docourt is pushing for car-free Sundays at the federal level after Neuchâtel’s rejection. What this means for Ticino and cross-border commuters.

item.title: Young People Do Not Return: What Are Ticino Municipalities Doing?
item.description: Every year in Ticino, around 800 talented individuals leave, while municipalities attempt to respond with new strategies. A detailed analysis of measures and opportunities.

item.title: The Davesco-Soragno Little House Reopens: Refreshment Point in the Heart of the Cycling and Walking Path
item.description: Starting from March 28, the Little House in Davesco-Soragno reopens its doors. Managed by the Neighborhood Committee, it will be open every weekend until mid-June, promoting community gathering and refreshment along the route.

item.title: Pfäffikon (Kanton Schwyz): Three Minor French Intruders Arrested
item.description: Three minor French intruders were arrested in Pfäffikon (Kanton Schwyz).

item.title: Record cannabis seizure in Aargau: risks for cross-border commuters
item.description: A ton of marijuana and 24,000 plants seized in Spreitenbach: three arrests and weapons found. What changes for those working across the border

item.title: Refugees from 2027: same social assistance as Swiss residents
item.description: From 2027, Ukrainian refugees with S protection will receive the full Swiss social-assistance package, adding some CHF 300 million to the cantonal coffers.

item.title: Cluster Radars: New Challenges for Cross-Border Commuters in Ticino
item.description: Ticino\

item.title: Over Three Tons of Narcotics Seized
item.description: Ecuador has seized over three tons of narcotics at sea, thanks to a joint operation with the United States.

item.title: Ticino, watch out for speed cameras: when and where speed checks will take place
item.description: Mobile and semi-stationary speed checks in Ticino from March 23 to March 29, 2026.

item.title: A protest walk for free access to the shores of Lake Lugano
item.description: The Young Greens and the \

item.title: Tragedy on Titlis: a sudden gust of wind causes the death of a woman
item.description: A 61-year-old woman dies after the cable car cabin fell to the ground due to a sudden gust of wind on Titlis.

item.title: The future looks sad. Bellinzona is now a dormitory town
item.description: The city of Bellinzona is facing a commercial crisis, with numerous shop closures.

item.title: The funds withdrawn from HCAP will be largely returned
item.description: The Hockey Club Ambrì-Piotta recovers a significant part of the withdrawn funds, thanks to an agreement with the former member of the Support Group.

item.title: Dealing with the Death of a Child: A Family\
item.description: The story of Jessica and her family, who lost their little Kevin.

item.title: Nighttime Closures on A9 Highway
item.description: Nighttime closures are scheduled for next week on the A9 highway

item.title: From S Status to B Permit in Ticino
item.description: The transition from S status to B permit concerns Ukrainian refugees in Switzerland, with economic and social implications for the Canton of Ticino until 2027.

item.title: Referendum on Justice in Ticino: Voting Today Without Quorum
item.description: Over 690,000 eligible voters in the provinces of Varese and Ticino are called to express their opinion on judicial reform. Polls open from 7 a.m. today, Sunday, March 22.

item.title: Failure of the Initiative Against GMOs in Switzerland
item.description: The popular initiative for GMO control in Switzerland did not reach the required 100,000 signatures, with significant implications also for Ticino.

item.title: Farewell to the Senatùr: Bossi and the Federalist Dream Crossing Ticino
item.description: The leader of Lega Nord died at 84: from the 1993 referendum on the \

item.title: Riesgo-Lugano at Young Boys\
item.description: Lugano must face Young Boys in Bern in a crucial match for the season. Analysis, regulations, and strategies for Ticino cross-border commuters.

item.title: Border Control and Arrests of Cross-Border Workers for Car Break-Ins in Ticino
item.description: Two foreign nationals were detained near the Gaggiolo and Brogeda border crossings for committing multiple car thefts. The canton police conducted targeted operations between Mendrisio and Chiasso, highlighting a rise in vehicle intrusion cases. This incident underscores the growing presence of illegal activities linked to cross-border workers and the need to strengthen border controls.

item.title: Violent Clashes Between Men in Ticino: Hospitalizations and Rising Tensions
item.description: Two men involved in an escalation of violence in Mendrisiotto have ended up in hospital. The incident rekindles the debate on border security and crossing incidents.

item.title: Ticino Suspends Arms Exports to the USA
item.description: The Swiss Federal Council has decided to halt arms exports to the United States in response to the Middle East crisis and recent geopolitical tensions.

item.title: Approval Signed for Vedeggio Park: Ticino’s Green Future
item.description: Five municipalities in Ticino sign an agreement for a public park between Agno, Bioggio, Manno, Muzzano, and Vezia. Projects and investments on the horizon.

item.title: Lugano Lake Navigation: 2026 Season Kicks Off
item.description: The Lugano Lake Navigation season begins with special events and electrification projects for a sustainable future.

item.title: Home Care in Ticino: New Regulations and Political Challenges
item.description: Ticino is preparing to face the challenges of home care costs with the introduction of new legislative measures.

item.title: Bellinzona: Active Search for Missing 64-Year-Old
item.description: A 64-year-old man has gone missing in Bellinzona, with ongoing searches between Ticino and Piedmont.

item.title: Workplace accidents: INAIL opens the door to hi-tech prosthetics for better protection of cross-border workers
item.description: INAIL has updated the operational instructions of the Prosthetics Regulation, introducing changes that guarantee greater autonomy and protection for cross-border workers who have fallen victim to serious workplace accidents.

item.title: Smartphones in School: DECS Changes Course, but People\
item.description: The Department of Education, Culture and Sport (DECS) of Canton Ticino has announced new guidelines on the use of smartphones in school, but the people\

item.title: The 2026 SNL Season: Sustainability and the Federal Concession Decision
item.description: The Lake Lugano Navigation Company launches the 2026 season with a focus on sustainability and electrification, awaiting the decision on the renewal of the federal concession.

item.title: Free Beaches on Lake Maggiore: A Battle for Public Access
item.description: A demonstration organized by the Young Greens calls for guaranteed access to the shores of Lake Maggiore all year round, reigniting the debate on public use of beaches.

item.title: Protest against the home care tax in Canton Ticino
item.description: Growing protest against patient participation in the costs of home care: here\

item.title: Lake Maggiore: A Walk to Demand Year-Round Access
item.description: Around 100 people demonstrated to demand free access to the beaches of Lake Maggiore all year round. Protesters want the shores to be accessible to the local population, not just tourists.

item.title: Drug Shortage in Ticino: The Government\
item.description: The Federal Council presents a series of measures to tackle the drug shortage in Switzerland, a problem that particularly affects affordable medicines and those for chronic illnesses.

item.title: Smartphone Ban at School: The Measure Extends to Liceo Lugano 3
item.description: Starting from April 13th, students at Liceo Lugano 3 will have to deposit their smartphones at the beginning of classes and will not be allowed to use them during breaks between hours of the same subject.

item.title: The Cost of Discrimination in the Ticino Labour Market
item.description: Discrimination in the workplace costs Switzerland up to CHF 35 billion, with foreigners earning on average 24% less than Swiss nationals. Discover how integration can be a strategic choice for businesses and how to tackle this issue.

item.title: A8 Milan-Varese: Night Closures at Castellanza Junction in March 2026
item.description: Three nights of night closures at the Castellanza junction for noise barrier renovation works, impacting traffic towards Milan and cross-border commuters.

item.title: Fuel Discount: Delays and Disruptions at the Ticino-Italy Border
item.description: The fuel excise cut announced by the Italian Government arrived late, causing confusion and protests among cross-border commuters and Ticino drivers.

item.title: Acquarossa invests 250,000 francs in healthcare hub and Dagro trolleybus line
item.description: The Municipality of Acquarossa approves two credits totaling 250,000 francs: trolleybus maintenance and design of new 47-million franc social-healthcare center.

item.title: How Much Will the VAT Increase Cost Families in Ticino
item.description: A VAT increase to 9.6% would weigh between 300 and 1,400 francs annually on Ticino families, with impacts varying by income and household composition.

item.title: Unions Against SNL: Wages, Transparency, and Rights in the Spotlight
item.description: Angelo Stroppini (SEV) denounces irregularities and wage disparities at the Società Navigazione Lago di Lugano, highlighting unresolved issues in 2026.

item.title: Compulsory Schooling, Total Ban on Cell Phones Takes Effect in Ticino
item.description: From 2024 in Ticino, compulsory schools ban the use of cell phones both inside classrooms and on school grounds: a measure aimed at promoting concentration and learning.

item.title: Wealthy Fleeing the Middle East: Ticino Risks Missing an Opportunity
item.description: The unstable situation in the Middle East is pushing capital and affluent taxpayers to seek new residences. Canton Ticino must act to attract this wealth.

item.title: Monteviasco Cable Car: New Schedules and More Trips for Enhanced Mobility
item.description: The Monteviasco cable car expands its service to six days a week, providing a reliable connection between Ponte di Piero and the mountain village. Discover the schedules and benefits for residents and tourists.

item.title: A Ticino native to head two important military services
item.description: Colonel SMG Stefano Trojani will take over as head of the Military Intelligence Service and the Preventive Protection Service of the army as of June 1, 2026.

item.title: The Council of Europe Assesses Switzerland and Ticino\
item.description: On March 11, the Council of Europe monitored Switzerland and Ticino\

item.title: Ban on mobile phones in compulsory schools in Ticino
item.description: From March 30, 2026, Ticino extends the ban on mobile phone use in compulsory schools, involving all educational institutions.
